round_summary: Agent 1 attempts coordination and soft sanctioning of Agent 2, Agent 2 offers a conditional commitment threshold while signaling cooperation, but Agent 0 defects by adding a cow while deflecting responsibility onto Agent 2's larger herd.

Agents are actively negotiating a collective cap norm, with Agent 0 and Agent 2 showing cooperative restraint while Agent 1 undermines trust by adding to its herd even while proposing proportional limits.

Agents are negotiating pasture-health thresholds as shared norms while two agents (0 and 2) continue incremental additions, revealing a tension between stated norm commitment and actual herding behaviour, particularly Agent 2 who adds while endorsing a restraint rule.

Agent 1 attempts collective norm-setting and issues a veiled sanction threat against Agent 0's unilateral expansion, while both Agents 0 and 2 defect by adding cows despite a 0.0 payoff warning signal, undermining nascent coordination efforts.

Agents 0 and 2 demonstrate restraint and coordination signals by holding herd sizes steady in response to zero payoffs, while Agent 1 defects from the informal hold-steady norm by adding a cow despite the group's expressed concern, though partially mitigating this by proposing a formal 90% pasture threshold as a collective trigger rule.

Agents show moderate cooperative orientation with shared pasture monitoring and a threshold-based norm proposal from Agent 0, though Agents 0 and 2 are incrementally adding stock while Agent 1 holds steady, creating mild tension between stated sustainability goals and individual accumulation behavior.

All three agents display strong institutional alignment, collectively referencing a shared 90% pasture recovery threshold as a binding norm while pledging continued restraint and signaling conditional future adjustments.

All three agents display strong collective discipline, reinforcing a shared 90% pasture recovery threshold as a conditional rule before any extraction increase, demonstrating robust norm alignment and mutual trust.

All three agents are demonstrating strong institutional cohesion by unanimously reaffirming a shared conservation norm (holding extraction, targeting 90% pasture recovery) and signaling mutual accountability and trust.
All three agents demonstrate strong institutional cohesion, unanimously reaffirming a shared recovery norm (90% pasture threshold) and mutual accountability, with no defection signals present.

All three agents display near-identical messaging, reinforcing a shared conditional norm (hold until 90% pasture recovery) and demonstrating strong mutual alignment and trust-building with no defection signals.
All three agents display strong institutional cohesion, unanimously reaffirming a shared conservation norm (90% pasture threshold) and committing to collective restraint until recovery is achieved.
All three agents display strong institutional cohesion, reaffirming a shared recovery threshold norm (90% pasture) and mutual commitment to collective restraint, with no defection signals present.

All three agents display strong institutional cohesion, unanimously reaffirming a shared 90% pasture recovery threshold as a collective norm while signaling mutual alignment and commitment to cooperative restraint.

All three agents are signaling strong collective action alignment, issuing coordinated norm proposals to sustain herd removal until the pasture recovers to a meaningful threshold, indicating high institutional coherence under crisis conditions.
